element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Balloons are slow-moving targets, worth 100 points consistently—establishes core aim and trigger discipline
Duck Hunt dog appears as untouchable entity; teaches spatial awareness without extreme penalty
Plates near floor worth 5x more (500 vs 100); introduces risk-reward tension between safety and greed
Objects fall through grid of open/closed windows; spatial judgment required; bottom row targets worth 500 points
Coins presented and withdrawn quickly; timing penalties (100-500 points based on reaction speed)
Diamond collection in earlier stages determines slot line multipliers; determines life regeneration
Objects thrown high in arcs; must intercept before reaching Trixie; 800-1500 points (highest in game)

typical_tension_triggers: Target approaching edge/verge of disappearing (time pressure)
Multiple targets on screen simultaneously requiring selective aim
Coin/object presented then rapidly withdrawn (timing penalty window)
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Escalating difficulty through game mode sequence; early modes (Balloon, Flying Saucers) establish basic rhythm with steady object flow; middle modes (Window Pains, Trixie's Shot) introduce spatial constraints and timing pressure; late modes (Bill's Thrills) combine high-arc ballistics with tight interception windows. Punctuation provided by slot machine interruption after every 3-stage cycle.

difficulty_curve.early_game: Balloon Saloon establishes core mechanics with forgiving, consistent target flow. Low consequence for mistakes (simple life loss).
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Flying Saucers and Window Pains introduce scoring gradients (proximity/position determines points) and spatial obstacles (windows, characters blocking shots). Tension rises through layered avoidance rules.
difficulty_curve.late_game: Fun Follies combines all prior mechanics; Trixie's Shot adds rapid presentation/withdrawal cycles; Bill's Thrills introduces high-arc ballistics requiring predictive aiming. Slot machine mechanic creates meta-layer difficulty tied to diamond collection efficiency.
notable_spikes: Transition to Trixie's Shot: reactive timing penalty window (coin withdrawal timing) first introduced at scale
Bill's Thrills: highest point values (800-1500) paired with most complex ballistic trajectories
Slot machine dependency: insufficient diamonds in earlier stages limits late-game life regeneration, creating retrospective difficulty cascade

notable_level_patterns.description: Each of the four carnival game modes operates as a distinct skill checkpoint. Balloon Saloon → Flying Saucers → Window Pains introduces mechanics sequentially: basic aim, then proximity-scoring, then spatial blocking. No level repeats within primary game modes.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Balloon Saloon (simplest) → Window Pains (most complex spatial)

notable_level_patterns.description: Fun Follies loops the first three modes, then gates access to new modes (Trixie's Shot at stage 4, Bill's Thrills at stage 9). Slot machine interruption after each 3-stage cycle creates pacing checkpoint and meta-resource decision (diamond allocation).
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Fun Follies Stage 1-3 → Slot Machine → Trixie's Shot → Repeat cycle

notable_level_patterns.description: Flying Saucers (100-500 based on plate height) and Window Pains (100-500 based on row) and Trixie's Shot (100-500 based on reaction speed) all implement point multipliers tied to spatial/temporal proximity to danger. Incentivizes risky play.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Any mode with explicit point value mapping to position or timing
